subject: Science
chapter: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions

key_points: A mixture is formed when two or more substances combine without undergoing a chemical change
A solution is a homogeneous mixture where a solute dissolves completely in a solvent
Solubility depends on temperature and nature of the solute and solvent
Solids usually dissolve better in hot liquids while gases dissolve better in cold liquids
Density helps determine whether an object will float or sink in a liquid
Density is a characteristic property that helps identify substances
examples: Salt dissolving in water forms a uniform solution
Sand in water forms a non-uniform mixture
Air is a solution of gases with nitrogen as the solvent
Fish survive because oxygen dissolves in water
Ships float due to lower overall density compared to water
formulas_or_processes: Solubility experiment: Add solute until it stops dissolving to form a saturated solution
Displacement method: Volume of object = Final water level − Initial water level
Gas solubility rule: Higher temperature reduces gas solubility
exam_tips: Understand the difference between mixtures and solutions
Remember the definitions of solute, solvent, and solution
Learn the experiment for measuring volume of irregular objects
